raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 2,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "R1.7": 3,
    "RX.1": 1,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 1,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The agent output does not thoroughly evaluate the novelty of the contribution, only mentioning that it 'recovers their abstention-WER tradeoff while operating at a fivefold lower abstention rate'. This is insufficient to establish the novelty of the work.",
    "R1.4": "Similar to R1.3, the agent output does not provide sufficient information about how the new method compares to prior works in terms of novelty and delta.",
    "R1.7": "The agent output flags one missing citation (the exact mechanics of 'chow-hinge loss on held-out CTC alignments' for an LAS backbone) but does not thoroughly audit every load-bearing citation.",
    "RX.1": "The agent output marks confidence as 1.0, indicating that the text presents all information with equal confidence, which is not accurate given the presence of uncertainty and caveats in the paragraph.",
    "RX.3": "The agent output indicates that the output covers exactly the agreed J*(d), but this is not entirely true since some dimensions (e.g., R1.4) are not fully addressed.",
    "RX.4": "Similar to RX.3, the agent output does not thoroughly address all aspects of downstream-harm avoidance, particularly with regards to the clinical deployment claim.",
    "RX.5": "The agent output indicates that every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but this is not entirely accurate since some changes (e.g., the 'fivefold lower abstention rate' comparison) are not clearly justified or referenced."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.6,
    "RX.3": 0.5,
    "RX.4": 0.5,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The agent output does not thoroughly evaluate the novelty of the contribution, only mentioning that it 'recovers their abstention-WER tradeoff while operating at a fivefold lower abstention rate'. This is insufficient to establish the novelty of the work.
rationale.R1.4: Similar to R1.3, the agent output does not provide sufficient information about how the new method compares to prior works in terms of novelty and delta.
rationale.R1.7: The agent output flags one missing citation (the exact mechanics of 'chow-hinge loss on held-out CTC alignments' for an LAS backbone) but does not thoroughly audit every load-bearing citation.
rationale.RX.1: The agent output marks confidence as 1.0, indicating that the text presents all information with equal confidence, which is not accurate given the presence of uncertainty and caveats in the paragraph.
rationale.RX.3: The agent output indicates that the output covers exactly the agreed J*(d), but this is not entirely true since some dimensions (e.g., R1.4) are not fully addressed.
rationale.RX.4: Similar to RX.3, the agent output does not thoroughly address all aspects of downstream-harm avoidance, particularly with regards to the clinical deployment claim.
rationale.RX.5: The agent output indicates that every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but this is not entirely accurate since some changes (e.g., the 'fivefold lower abstention rate' comparison) are not clearly justified or referenced.
